title: Genomic DNA Extraction from Animal Faecal Tissues Using DNeasy Kit
author: Somasundhari Shanmuganandam
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6bbhain

steps.instruction: Thaw the samples in ice and bring it to room temperature
Using a scapel, place the tissue in a petri dish and cut 50 mg of tissue. Check the tissue weight to confirm. Place it in a 2 ml eppendorf tube filled with 0.5 cm of purified silica beads.
Add 324 μl of buffer ATL and 36 μl of proteinase K into each of the bead tubes containing sample. For reagent only control, take empty bead tube and add only buffer ATL and proteinase K.
Place the bead tubes into the bead beater for 2 x 20 sec and spin it down.
Incubate the samples at 56°C in heat block for 10 min to 30 min. Actual time depends on tissue lysis
Add 4 μl of RNase A (100 mg/ml) to the lysed samples, vortex and incubate for 5 min at room temperature.
Vortex and transfer 200 μl of the sample solution into a new 1.5 ml eppendorf tube.
Transfer the supernatant (as much as possible) into a new 1.5 ml eppendorf tube and discard the pellet. Note: The supernatant may contain tiny tissue fragments which can be lysed in the next step
Add 200 μl of buffer AL and vortex.
Add 200 μl of ethanol pipetting up and down. Vortex. Note : Step 10 must be done immediately after step 9
Tranfer the entire content of the eppendorf tube into a spin column provided with the kit.
Centrifuge at 6,000 rcf for 1 min. Discard the flow through and collection tube.
Place a new collection tube under the spin column and add 500 μl of buffer AW1
Centrifuge at 6,000 rcf for 1 min. Discard the flow through and collection tube.
Place a new collection tube under the spin column and add 500 μl of buffer AW2.
Centrifuge at maximum speed (13,000 rcf) for 3 mins
Discard the flow through and place the same collection tube back into the column. Perform centrifuge for 1 min at maximum speed
Discard collection tube and place a 1.5 ml eppendorf tube under the column and add 100 μl of buffer AE. Let it stand for 1 min.
Centrifuge at 6000 rcf for 1 min.
Pipette the eluted DNA from the eppenforf tube and add it into the column again for re-elution
Let it stand for one min and centrifuge at 6000 rcf for 1 min.
Discard the filter column. Store 50 μl of eluted DNA at 4°C for quantitative analysis and store the rest at -20°C to prevent repeated freeze-thaw cycle.
Prepare 0.8% agarose gel and run at 100 V for 40-45 min to check the quality of DNA extracted
Make sure the qubit is standardised prior to starting
Use qubit broad range DNA assay kit and perform analysis using 1 μl of extracted DNA. Note down the concentration value (ng/μl)
Use 2 μl of distilled water as blank and 2 μl of buffer AE as standard. Clean the surface between each read.
Add 2 μl of sample DNA into the surface of the nanodrop and read. Clean the surface before next use. Note down the A260/280, A260/230 and concentration (ng/μl)
